chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, this video is about asymptotic notations.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Asymptotic notations are used for representing the time complexity of algorithms.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If we analyze the algorithms by studying and finding the frequency of execution of their

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: statements, then we may get the time complexities, anyone among these, like the time complexity

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: may be constant or it may be logarithmic, so that is in terms of log n or linear or

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: it may even be n log n or it may be quadratic n square n cube 2 power n.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So these are some of the time complexities, known time complexities.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Any algorithm we take, the time complexity of that algorithm may be anyone among these

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: or the combination of these, multiples of these.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So before going to asymptotic notations, let us look at few algorithms and try to find

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the time complexities for them.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Method for finding the time complexities, we will find the frequency of execution of

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: statements, like in this algorithm, there is only one statement and the time for execution

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is 1, so it is constant.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you say f of n is the function for time, then we get the value as 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this algorithm, if you take, this statement will execute for one time, this will execute

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: for n plus 1 time and this is n times, this one will execute for one time.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This for loop will execute for n plus 1 time because 1 to n, then when it is n plus 1,

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that time it will stop.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So total n plus 1 times.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this gives 2n plus 3.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So when we find the time complexity by just studying the algorithm state by state statement,

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we get the time in the form of a polynomial.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Right, so 2n plus 3, this, if you see the degree of polynomial, the highest degree is

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n, so the time complexity is linear.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you take this algorithm, this is n plus 1, this is inside the for loop, so this is

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n and this itself will execute for n plus 1 times and this will execute for n square

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: times.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will be 2n square plus 2n plus 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So f of n is 2n square plus 2n plus 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The highest degree of polynomial is n square, so the time complexity is quadratic.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you take this algorithm for matrix multiplication, this is n plus 1, this is n into n plus 1,

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is 1, sorry, this is n square times and this is n square into n plus 1 and this

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is n cube.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 2n cube plus 3n square plus 2n plus 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the function of time is so and so.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We got this polynomial and the highest degree is n cube, so the time complexity is cubic.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have binary search algorithm.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is 1, this is 1 and this loop will repeat as long as i is less than j.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So i is 1 and j is n and it's going to divide i plus j by 2 every time, so it will be taking

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the middle.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it will be dividing the indices i to j by 2.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will repeat for log n time, entire thing.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So all the statement inside this will execute for log n time.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Either this or this statement will execute, any one of them.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So finally it is 3 log n plus 2.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we got the function as so and so.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the highest degree.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is logarithmic.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have taken a recursive function.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If the time for this is Tn, this is 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be Tn because it's a recursive call.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is calling itself for n minus 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is also Tn minus 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So total time is 2Tn minus 1 plus 1.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is a recurrence relation.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you solve this recurrence relation, then we get the time as 2 power n.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Highest degree in a function will be 2 power n.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I'll not show how to solve this one.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: You can watch it in other videos.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 2 power n.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time complexity is exponential.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we can say that the time complexities will be one among these constant, logarithmic,

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: linear, quadratic, or multiple of these.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have taken all those time complexities in the increasing order.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now if for any algorithm, we got the time complexity as f of n is equals 2n square plus

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3n plus 2.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose we got this as a polynomial.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The highest degree in this one is n square.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then we say that all these time complexities including n square becomes upper bound of

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: function f of n.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And all these time complexities including n square becomes lower bound.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And n square itself becomes tight bound.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now here.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it means we can say f of n is big O of n square.

chunks.text: So even f of n can be written as big O of n cube.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: All the time complexities smaller than or equal to n square comes under lower bound.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now n square is coming in both.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this becomes tight bound.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It means I am making asymptotic notations very simple.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here it means that f of n is big O of n square.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So big O is used for upper bound.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: f of n can also be called as big O of n cube and even big O of 2 power n.

chunks.title: Notations - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I can take any of the time complexities as upper bound that are greater than or equal
